approval_notes: Owner selected 'Full task-004 in one slice (Recommended)' for the scope decision and then 'Approve - implement slice 2 now', authorising the full task-004 domain model as a single atomic increment.

This is the SECOND of three F003 slices. Slice 1 (configuration system, CODEX task 003 = F003 acceptance step 1) is merged to main (PR #9, squash 3b7e0b1). Slice 2 = the domain model + Alembic migrations (CODEX task 004 = F003 acceptance steps 2 and 3). Slice 3 = the Z0 classification engine (task 005 = acceptance step 4).

F003 stays passes:false after this slice. Flipping F003 passes:true requires the FULL epic (config + domain model + Z0 engine) plus a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 evaluation, exactly as F002 was closed out.
No new runtime dependency: SQLAlchemy 2.0.36 and Alembic 1.14.0 are already pinned in pyproject.toml and apps/api/requirements.txt.
objective: Introduce the FreeTier Atlas catalogue/evidence domain model as SQLAlchemy 2.0 declarative ORM models and a forward-and-reversible Alembic migration (0003). Enforce referential integrity (foreign keys), closed-vocabulary check constraints drawn from docs/DATA_MODEL.md (zero-cost classes, offer types, exhaustion behaviours, change types, etc.), append-only immutability of offer_version rows via a database trigger, and full evidence provenance (Evidence references a Source, an OfferVersion, and a Snapshot). The migration must apply, roll back, and re-apply cleanly against PostgreSQL, and the ORM metadata must be wired into Alembic (target_metadata) so schema/model drift can be detected.
scope: New package apps/api/app/models/: base.py (DeclarativeBase with a deterministic naming_convention + shared metadata), domain.py (the 13 entities: Provider, Category, Service, Offer, OfferVersion, Quota, RegionAvailability, Source, Snapshot, Evidence, ChangeEvent, ScanRun, ReviewItem), vocab.py (closed-vocabulary tuples mirroring docs/DATA_MODEL.md), __init__.py exports.

migrations/versions/0003_domain_model.py: creates all 13 tables with FKs, check constraints, indexes, and a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger + function that makes offer_version append-only; downgrade() drops the trigger/function and all tables in dependency order.
migrations/env.py: import the domain metadata and set target_metadata = Base.metadata so autogenerate / compare_metadata drift checks work.

tests/integration/test_domain_migration.py (integration-marked, gated on DATABASE_URL): apply head, assert the 13 tables + trigger exist, assert compare_metadata reports no drift, exercise FK + check-constraint rejections, assert offer_version UPDATE and DELETE are rejected by the trigger, run representative provenance queries, then downgrade to 0002 (assert domain tables gone) and upgrade head again (round trip).

tests/unit/test_domain_models.py (offline): assert the ORM metadata exposes the 13 tables, the closed-vocabulary check constraints are present with the exact docs/DATA_MODEL.md membership, evidence carries the three provenance foreign keys, and the models import without a database.

scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 and scripts/stack-smoke.sh: add checks asserting the domain tables exist (e.g. provider, offer, offer_version, evidence) and that the offer_version immutability trigger is installed, run against the live stack Postgres.
docs/DATA_MODEL.md: append an 'Implementation' section cross-referencing the ORM module, the 0003 migration, the immutability trigger, and how to apply/roll back.
out_of_scope: The Z0 classification engine and truth table (task 005) -> F003 slice 3.
Provider adapters, source scanning, document fetching, evidence extraction, or any network I/O.
API routes or worker/scheduler logic that read or write the new tables at runtime; seeding real catalogue/offer data.
Runtime consumption of slice-1 configuration by services.
Marking F003 (or any feature) passes:true; adding managed-cloud services or heavyweight dependencies; advancing past this slice.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 domain entities (Provider, Category, Service, Offer, OfferVersion, Quota, RegionAvailability, Source, Snapshot, Evidence, ChangeEvent, ScanRun, ReviewItem) are defined as SQLAlchemy declarative models on a shared metadata with a deterministic naming convention.
Alembic 'upgrade head' applies migration 0003 cleanly against PostgreSQL, creating all 13 tables with their foreign keys, check constraints, and indexes.

Round trip: 'downgrade 0002' removes every domain table, function, and trigger, and a subsequent 'upgrade head' recreates the identical schema (verified by alembic compare_metadata reporting no drift after re-apply).
offer_version is append-only: an UPDATE or a DELETE against an existing offer_version row is rejected by a database trigger (raises an exception); INSERT of a new version succeeds.

Evidence provenance is FK-enforced: an Evidence row requires a valid source_id, offer_version_id, and snapshot_id; inserting Evidence that references a non-existent source/offer_version/snapshot is rejected.

Closed-vocabulary check constraints reject out-of-vocabulary values for zero_cost_class (5 classes), offer_type (11 types), and exhaustion_behaviour (12 behaviours), using exactly the membership listed in docs/DATA_MODEL.md.
migrations/env.py sets target_metadata to the domain metadata; an integration test confirms compare_metadata reports no operations after 'upgrade head' (migration and models agree).
Representative queries succeed: joining Provider -> Service -> Offer -> OfferVersion and OfferVersion -> Evidence -> Source return expected rows for seeded fixture data inside the test.

scripts/stack-smoke.{ps1,sh} pass against the live stack including new assertions that the domain tables exist and the offer_version immutability trigger is installed; a full stack-up / stack-smoke / stack-down cycle succeeds.

Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 (pytest offline + config validation + web tests/build) and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit (ruff, format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit), including the requirements-sync guard (no new dependency).
No unsupported free-tier/zero-cost/Z0 product claim is introduced (schema and constraints only; no offer data asserting a service is free).
A fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator records an adversarial verdict in agent-state/evaluation.json before any status change is claimed; F003 remains passes:false (epic incomplete).

integration_commands: $env:DATABASE_URL to the live stack Postgres; .venv pytest -m integration tests/integration/test_domain_migration.py
end_to_end_steps: Bring up the stack; confirm the API entrypoint applied 0003 (domain tables present via psql).

Run the integration migration test: apply head, assert 13 tables + trigger, compare_metadata clean, FK/check rejections, offer_version UPDATE/DELETE rejected, provenance query, downgrade 0002, upgrade head again.
Run scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 and confirm the new domain-table and immutability-trigger checks pass alongside the existing API/worker/scheduler/web checks.
Run scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit and confirm both pass end-to-end.
Confirm detect-secrets is green and no credential appears in code, migration, or output.
negative_tests: UPDATE offer_version SET ... is rejected by the immutability trigger with a raised exception.
DELETE FROM offer_version WHERE ... is rejected by the immutability trigger.
INSERT into offer with zero_cost_class='ZX' (not in the 5-class vocabulary) is rejected by a check constraint.
INSERT into offer with offer_type='freebie' (not in the 11-type vocabulary) is rejected.
INSERT into quota with exhaustion_behaviour='melts' (not in the 12-behaviour vocabulary) is rejected.
INSERT into evidence referencing a non-existent snapshot_id/source_id/offer_version_id is rejected by a foreign key.

risks.data_migration: This slice introduces the first real domain migration (0003). It is additive (creates new tables) and fully reversible; downgrade drops the trigger, function, and tables in dependency order. The pre-existing app_meta scaffold table is left untouched (superseded logically, not dropped, to keep 0001/0002 round trips intact).
risks.security_privacy: Schema only; no credentials. The migration hardcodes no connection string (env.py reads DATABASE_URL). No secret values are logged.
risks.portability: No managed-cloud dependency. Uses standard PostgreSQL DDL (tables, FKs, check constraints, one PL/pgSQL trigger function). Builds on amd64 and arm64; SQLAlchemy/Alembic already present.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The check-constraint vocabularies mirror docs/DATA_MODEL.md exactly. No offer row asserting a service is free is seeded; the schema is empty of product claims.
risks.regression: No dependency change, so the requirements-sync guard is unaffected. The immutability trigger only affects offer_version; existing 0001/0002 tables and their tests are unchanged.
risks.supply_chain: No new dependency (Python or npm) is added by this slice.

notes: Owner approved the full task-004 domain-model scope as one slice and authorised implementation ('Approve - implement slice 2 now'). This slice does not mark F003 passing; it is the second of three F003 slices and requires an independent Level 2 evaluation covering migration, constraints, immutability, rollback, and representative queries before its completion is claimed.
